title: Fixing React Memoized Component Re-renders on Object Reference Change

statement.markdown: Create a memoized React component that displays a person's name and age. The component should only re-render when the person's name or age has changed. However, the current implementation re-renders on every change, even if the person remains the same but the object reference has changed. Fix the bug to optimize the re-renders.
statement.inputFormat: The function takes an object with 'name' and 'age' properties.
statement.outputFormat: The function returns a React memoized component.
statement.notes: Time and space complexity should be optimized.

constraints.description: Number of times the component is re-rendered
Number of times the person object is reassigned

examples.explanation: Person remains the same, no re-render
Person remains the same, no re-render
Person remains the same, no re-render

hints.text: Use the `React.memo` component to memoize the component's props.
Use the `areEqual` function to compare the person object's reference.

editorial.title: Optimizing React Memoized Component Re-renders
editorial.intuition: The `React.memo` component can be used to memoize the component's props, but it requires a custom comparison function to avoid re-renders on object reference changes.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible brute-force approach is to use `JSON.stringify` to compare the person object's properties.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution is to use a custom comparison function that checks for object reference changes.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use `React.memo` with a custom comparison function that checks for both object reference and property changes.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correct solution will optimize the re-renders and match the expected output.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Create a person object, re-render the component, and verify the output.
commonMistakes: Not providing a custom comparison function
